concepts.finding:
We introduce BloomIntent, a
user-centric search evaluation method that uses user intents as the
evaluation unit.
concepts.implication: Can inform intent modeling for recommendation systems

concepts.finding:
We introduce BloomIntent, a multi-intent evaluation
method that simulates a wide spectrum of plausible user intents for
a given query, and automatically evaluates how well each is fulfilled
by a search engine result page (SERP).
concepts.implication: Can inform intent modeling for recommendation systems

concepts.finding:
Our contributions are as follows:
• BloomIntent, a novel LLM-powered evaluation method
that generates and contextualizes diverse, fine-grained user
intents to enable automatic, intent-level evaluation of search
results.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
Research has shown that LLMs
can predict searcher preferences at levels comparable to human
annotators [69] and provide nuanced, context-aware evaluations
for document ranking [51].

8
Conclusion
In this paper, we introduce BloomIntent, a search evaluation
method that centers on user intents as the core unit of analysis.
concepts.implication: Can inform intent modeling for recommendation systems
